message:
fix(tools): add file size guard and fix empty-file handling in hashline_edit

Two fixes to the hashline_edit tool:

1. File size guard: Replace fs::read_to_string() with File::open() +
   .take(READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES + 1) to enforce the same 100MB file size
   limit used by the Read tool. Previously, hashline_edit would attempt
   to load arbitrarily large files into memory, risking OOM on huge
   files.

2. Empty-file edge case: When operating on an empty file (file_lines
   == [""]), "prepend" and "append" operations without an explicit
   position marker now correctly resolve as "replace" of the single
   empty line rather than inserting around it. This prevents a stray
   blank line artifact that would appear when prepending/appending to
   an empty file. Additionally, the EOF anchor for "append" without a
   position now accounts for trailing empty lines from a final newline,
   landing the insertion at the last non-empty line instead of after
   the trailing empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
